title: PHP Array Sorting
content:
### PHP Array Sorting

PHP provides several sorting functions that work on arrays.

### Sort Functions

| Function | Sorts by | Maintains key association |
|----------|----------|--------------------------|
| `sort()` | Values | No (re-indexes) |
| `rsort()` | Values (reverse) | No |
| `asort()` | Values | Yes |
| `arsort()` | Values (reverse) | Yes |
| `ksort()` | Keys | Yes |
| `krsort()` | Keys (reverse) | Yes |
| `usort()` | Custom comparison | No |
| `uasort()` | Custom comparison | Yes |
| `uksort()` | Keys (custom) | Yes |
| `natsort()` | Natural order | Yes |

```php
<?php
$fruits = ["Banana", "Apple", "Cherry", "Date"];
sort($fruits);  // ["Apple", "Banana", "Cherry", "Date"]
rsort($fruits); // ["Date", "Cherry", "Banana", "Apple"]

$ages = ["Alice" => 25, "Bob" => 30, "Charlie" => 22];
asort($ages);  // Maintains key => value association
ksort($ages);  // Sorts by keys
?>
```

### Sorting Flags

| Flag | Description |
|------|-------------|
| `SORT_REGULAR` | Normal comparison |
| `SORT_NUMERIC` | Numeric comparison |
| `SORT_STRING` | String comparison |
| `SORT_NATURAL` | Natural order (natsort) |
| `SORT_FLAG_CASE` | Case-insensitive (with SORT_STRING or SORT_NATURAL) |

> **Key Point:** `sort()` and `rsort()` re-index the array numerically. Use `asort()` and `arsort()` if you need to preserve key-value associations.
codeExample:
85, "Bob" => 92, "Charlie" => 78, "Diana" => 95 ]; // Sort by value (descending), maintain keys arsort($students); echo "Sorted by score (high to low):
"; foreach ($students as $name => $score) { echo "$name: $score
"; } // Sort by key ksort($students); echo "
Sorted by name:
"; foreach ($students as $name => $score) { echo "$name: $score
"; } ?>

quiz.question: Which sort function maintains key-value association?
Which flag sorts numbers correctly as numbers?
